questions.text: Carbonates of sodium are stable but carbonates of lithium are less stable because of?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electronegativity affects bond formation with electrons, but it does not significantly impact the stability of ionic compounds like carbonates.

Lithium ions (Li⁺) have a high charge density due to their smaller size, leading to stronger interactions with carbonate ions (CO₃²⁻), which makes lithium carbonates less stable compared to sodium carbonates.
This is incorrect because lithium ions have a higher charge density compared to sodium ions, leading to less stable carbonates.
This is incorrect, as the reason for the difference in stability between lithium and sodium carbonates is well understood in terms of charge density.

questions.explanation: Lithium ion (Li⁺) is very small with a high charge density. This high charge density polarizes the carbonate ion (CO₃²⁻) strongly, making lithium carbonate less stable. Sodium ions (Na⁺) are larger with lower charge density, so their carbonates are more stable.

questions.text: Ammonia may be prepared by heating ammonium chloride with:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This reactant is not used in this reaction.
This reactant is not used in this reaction.

Ammonium chloride which is a salt in nature will react with base sodium hydroxide to form ammonia gas, water and sodium chloride. Ammonium chloride will dissociate in water, it will not react with NaCl or dilute H2SO4. If a mixture of pure (solid) ammonium chloride and concentrated sulfuric acid is heated, hydrogen chloride gas will evolve.
This reactant is not used in this reaction.

Ammonium chloride will dissociate in water, it will not react with NaCl or dilute H2SO4. If a mixture of pure (solid) ammonium chloride and concentrated sulfuric acid is heated, hydrogen chloride gas will evolve.

questions.text: Which one of the following pairs show the diagonal relationship in the periodic table?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Sodium (Na) and lithium (Li) do not show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. While they are both alkali metals and share some similarities in their properties, they are not adjacent to each other in the periodic table and do not have the same electron configurations. Therefore, they do not exhibit the same kind of diagonal relationship as, for example, lithium and magnesium.

Lithium (Li) and magnesium (Mg) show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. This is because they have similar ionic radii and electronegativities despite being in different groups. This similarity is because both elements have a filled 2s subshell and an empty 3p subshell. As a result, they exhibit similar chemical and physical properties, such as high reactivity and the ability to form stable compounds with other elements. This diagonal relationship is an example of how the periodic table can be used to predict the properties of elements based on their position in the table.

Lithium (Li) and beryllium (Be) do not show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. While they are both in the same group (group 2) and share some similarities in their properties, they are not adjacent to each other in the periodic table and do not have the same electron configurations. Therefore, they do not exhibit the same kind of diagonal relationship as, for example, lithium and magnesium.

Boron (B) and beryllium (Be) do not show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. While they are both in the same period (period 2) and share some similarities in their properties, they are not adjacent to each other in the periodic table and do not have the same electron configurations. Therefore, they do not exhibit the same kind of diagonal relationship.

questions.explanation: Lithium (Li) and magnesium (Mg) show a diagonal relationship in the periodic table. This is because they have similar ionic radii and electronegativities despite being in different groups. This similarity is due to the fact that both elements have a filled 2s subshell and an empty 3p subshell. As a result, they exhibit similar chemical and physical properties, such as high reactivity and the ability to form stable compounds with other elements. This diagonal relationship is an example of how the periodic table can be used to predict the properties of elements based on their position in the table.

questions.text: The second ionization potential of alkali metals are very high due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This is not the correct explanation for the high second ionization potential of alkali metals.

Second ionization energy can be defined as the removal of one mole of electron from the one mole of monovalent gaseous cation of the element as after the first ionization process. Alkali metals contain only one valence electron. On losing this electron, these metals attain noble gas configuration. Therefore, the removal of a second electron requires a huge amount of energy as the electron has to be removed from the shell that is closer to the nucleus.
This is true, but the specific reason for the high second ionization potential is the attainment of an inert gas configuration rather than the ns1 electronic configuration itself.
Being metals does not directly relate to the high second ionization potential of alkali metals.

questions.explanation: Second ionization energy can be defined as the removal of one mole of electron from the one mole of monovalent gaseous cation of the element as after the first ionization process. Alkali metals contain only one valence electron. On losing this electron, these metals attain noble gas configuration. Therefore, the removal of a second electron requires a huge amount of energy as the electron has to be removed from the shell that is closer to the nucleus.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A substance that tends to bring about reduction by being oxidized and losing electrons is reducing agentMetals lose electrons when they react and make the other reactant gain electrons (reduction) hence they are "reducing agents".
A substance that tends to bring about oxidation by being reduced and gaining electrons is oxidizing agent
IncorrectOption B: A substance that tends to bring about oxidation by being reduced and gaining electrons is oxidizing agentNot the case with metals
A dehydrating agent is a substance that dries or removes water from a material. In chemical reactions where dehydration occurs, the reacting molecule loses a molecule of water.

questions.text: S-S bond is present in which of the ion pairs?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. S₂O₇²⁻ (disulfur heptoxide ion)No S–S bondThe sulfur atoms are connected via oxygen bridges, not directly bonded to each other.S₂O₃²⁻ (thiosulfate ion)Has an S–S bondOne sulfur is central (bonded to three oxygens), and the second sulfur is bonded directly to the first — forming an S–S bond

This option is incorrect. S₄O₆²⁻ (tetrathionate ion)Has two S–S bondsThe structure has a chain of four sulfur atoms, with S–S–S–S bonds, and oxygens bonded to the terminal sulfurs.S₂O₇²⁻ (disulfur heptoxide ion) No S–S bond The sulfur atoms are connected via oxygen bridges, not directly bonded to each other.

This option is incorrect. S₂O₇²⁻ (disulfur heptoxide ion)No S–S bondThe sulfur atoms are connected via oxygen bridges, not directly bonded to each other.S₂O₈²⁻ (peroxodisulfate ion)No S–S bondThe two sulfur atoms are linked by a peroxide bridge (–O–O–), not by a direct S–S bond.

S₄O₆²⁻ (tetrathionate ion)Has two S–S bondsThe structure has a chain of four sulfur atoms, with S–S–S–S bonds, and oxygens bonded to the terminal sulfurs.S₂O₃²⁻ (thiosulfate ion) Has an S–S bond One sulfur is central (bonded to three oxygens), and the second sulfur is bonded directly to the first — forming an S–S bond.

questions.text: Alkaline earth metal hydroxides decompose on heating. Which of the following reactions is a correct representation of this decomposition?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: When heated, alkaline earth metal hydroxides undergo thermal decomposition to produce the corresponding metal oxide and water. The reaction can be represented as:M(OH)2(s) → MO(s) + H2O(l)where M represents an alkaline earth metal.For example, when magnesium hydroxide is heated, it decomposes to produce magnesium oxide and water:Mg(OH)2(s) → MgO(s) + H2O(l)

When heated, alkaline earth metal hydroxides undergo thermal decomposition to produce the corresponding metal oxide and water. The reaction can be represented as:M(OH)2(s) → MO(s) + H2O(l)where M represents an alkaline earth metal.For example, when magnesium hydroxide is heated, it decomposes to produce magnesium oxide and water:Mg(OH)2(s) → MgO(s) + H2O(l)

When heated, alkaline earth metal hydroxides undergo thermal decomposition to produce the corresponding metal oxide and water. The reaction can be represented as:M(OH)2(s) → MO(s) + H2O(l)where M represents an alkaline earth metal.For example, when magnesium hydroxide is heated, it decomposes to produce magnesium oxide and water:Mg(OH)2(s) → MgO(s) + H2O(l)

When heated, alkaline earth metal hydroxides undergo thermal decomposition to produce the corresponding metal oxide and water. The reaction can be represented as:M(OH)2(s) → MO(s) + H2O(l)where M represents an alkaline earth metal.For example, when magnesium hydroxide is heated, it decomposes to produce magnesium oxide and water:Mg(OH)2(s) → MgO(s) + H2O(l)

questions.text: Ionic radius decreases along the period due to:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The addition of a new electron shell occurs when moving down a group, not across a period, and it increases the ionic radius rather than decreasing it.

Across a period, the nuclear charge increases while electron shielding remains relatively constant. This results in a stronger attraction between the nucleus and the electrons, pulling the electron cloud closer and reducing the ionic radius.
High ionization energy is a characteristic of atoms with a strong hold on their electrons, but it does not directly explain the decrease in ionic radius across a period.
A decrease in nuclear charge would result in a weaker attraction to the electrons and an increase in ionic radius, not a decrease.

questions.explanation: Across a period, effective nuclear charge increases as electron shielding remains constant. A higher effective nuclear charge causes greater attraction to the electrons, pulling the electron cloud closer to the nucleus which results in a smaller atomic radius.

questions.text: The cation that distort the electron cloud of NO3− ion more and facilitates its decomposition is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Li+ has a small size but a single positive charge. This results in relatively low charge density compared to doubly charged cations, limiting its ability to significantly polarize the NO3− ion.
Mg2+ has a larger ionic radius, resulting in a lower charge density compared to smaller cations with the same charge. This makes it less effective at polarizing the NO3− ion.
Cs+ has a very large ionic size and a single positive charge, leading to very low charge density, making it the least effective at polarizing NO3−.
Be2+ has a very high charge density due to its small size and double positive charge, making it the most effective at polarizing the NO3− ion, facilitating its decomposition.

questions.explanation: Polarizability refers to the ability of a cation to distort the electron cloud of an anion in an ionic compound and the anionic cloud is pulled towards the cation when it gets distorted. According to Fajan’s rule, the greater charge density(charge/size) on a cation leads to greater distortion. Due to higher charge and smaller size, the charge density of Be++ is and so it will distort the electron cloud of NO3- more facilitating its decomposition.

questions.text: The compound which can be hydrolyzed by means of water is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Since CCl4 does not have a polar functional group that can be attacked by water molecules, it cannot undergo hydrolysis. Hence, it is an incorrect option.

Silicon can be hydrolysed by water because the silicon atom of silicon tetrachloride has energetically suitable vacant d-orbitals. Thus, it can accept a lone pair of electrons from the oxygen atom of a water molecule. Therefore, a silicon atom can expand its coordination number beyond four. Due to this reason SiCl4 is hydrolysed.Silicon tetrachloride (SiCl4) can undergo hydrolysis reaction with water to form orthosilicic acid (H4SiO4) and hydrogen chloride (HCl) gas.SiCl4 + 4H2O → H4SiO4 + 4HCl

CH4 cannot be hydrolyzed by water because it is already fully saturated with hydrogen atoms and does not contain any reactive functional groups such as hydroxyl (-OH) or carbonyl (C=O) groups that can undergo hydrolysis reactions. Hence, it is an incorrect option.
It is an incorrect option.

questions.explanation: Silicon can be hydrolysed by water because the silicon atom of silicon tetrachloride has energetically suitable vacant d-orbitals. Thus, it can accept a lone pair of electrons from the oxygen atom of a water molecule. Therefore, a silicon atom can expand its coordination number beyond four. Due to this reason SiCl4 is hydrolysed.Silicon tetrachloride (SiCl4) can undergo hydrolysis reaction with water to form orthosilicic acid (H4SiO4) and hydrogen chloride (HCl) gas.SiCl4 + 4H2O → H4SiO4 + 4HCl

questions.text: What is the product when chlorine gas is passed over element silicon in powdered state and heated it produce colorless liquid having the formula?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: SiCI2 is incorrect because it is not a product of this reaction.

When chlorine gas is passed over powdered silicon and heated, it produces silicon tetrachloride (SiCl4), which is a colourless liquid.The balanced chemical equation for this reaction is:Si(s) + 2C12(g) → SiCl4(1)Therefore, the formula of the colourless liquid produced is SiCl4, which is silicon tetrachloride. Thus, the correct answer is option B: SiCl4.
Si2C13 is incorrect because it is a compound containing two silicon atoms and three chlorine atoms, but it is not produced by the reaction of chlorine gas with powdered silicon.
SiCI is incorrect because it is a hypothetical compound and does not exist in reality.

questions.explanation: When chlorine gas is passed over powdered silicon and heated, it produces silicon tetrachloride (SiCl4), which is a colourless liquid.The balanced chemical equation for this reaction is:Si(s) + 2C12(g) → SiCl4(1)Therefore, the formula of the colourless liquid produced is SiCl4, which is silicon tetrachloride. Thus, the correct answer is option B: SiCl4.

questions.text: Which one of the following is more soluble in water?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Be(OH)2 (Beryllium hydroxide):Beryllium hydroxide is relatively insoluble in water. Beryllium compounds tend to have low solubility in water due to the small size and high charge density of the beryllium ion.
b) Ca(OH)2 (Calcium hydroxide):Calcium hydroxide, also known as slaked lime, is sparingly soluble in water. It is more soluble than beryllium hydroxide but still has limited solubility.
c) Mg(OH)2 (Magnesium hydroxide):Magnesium hydroxide, also known as milk of magnesia, is moderately soluble in water. It has better solubility compared to beryllium and calcium hydroxides.
d) Ba(OH)2 (Barium hydroxide):Barium hydroxide is highly soluble in water. It is the most soluble among the options and can readily dissolve to form a strong alkaline solution.

questions.explanation: Group II metal hydroxides become more soluble in water as you go down the column(group). This trend can be explained by the decrease in the lattice energy of the hydroxide salt and by the increase in the coordination number of the metal ion as you go down the column.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest value of electronegativity?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.
The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.
The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

Elements from the halogen group including F, Cl, Br have pretty high electronegativities. The most electronegative element is Fluorine with a score of 4.0 (the highest possible.) Across from Fluorine we also have N and O with high electronegativities. Electronegativity is basically how much the elements 'want' the electrons. A simple way to think about it is that the closer an element is to Fluorine, the higher its electronegativity. The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

questions.explanation: Elements from the halogen group including F, Cl, Br have pretty high electronegativities. The most electronegative element is Fluorine with a score of 4.0 (the highest possible.) Across from Fluorine we also have N and O with high electronegativities. Electronegativity is basically how much the elements 'want' the electrons. A simple way to think about it is that the closer an element is to Fluorine, the higher its electronegativity. The correct order of electronegativity in Halogen group is: F>Cl>Br>I.

questions.text: Semi water gas is a mixture of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option incorrectly includes only carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H2) without nitrogen (N₂). Semi-water gas is a mixture of CO, H₂, and N₂.
This choice erroneously excludes hydrogen (H2) and only includes carbon monoxide (CO) and nitrogen (N2). CO, H2, and N2 are combined to form semi-water gas.

Correct! This option correctly identifies the components of semi-water gas, which includes carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen (H2), and nitrogen (N2). Semi-water gas is used as a fuel source in various industrial processes.
This option incorrectly suggests that none of the provided mixtures are correct, but in reality, the correct mixture for semi-water gas is CO + H₂ + N₂.

questions.explanation: Semi-water gas is produced when a mixture of steam and air is passed over hot coke. It consists of a mixture of water gas (CO + H₂) and producer gas (CO + N₂), resulting in the final mixture CO + H₂ + N₂.

questions.text: The carbonates of alkali metals are soluble in water except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Potassium Carbonate's interaction with water is a highly violent and exothermic process in which potassium carbonate is split into potassium and carbonate ions. Potassium Hydroxide and Carbonic Acid are formed when the ions interact.
Lithium carbonate is insoluble in water. Hence, Option B is correct.
When dissolved in water, sodium carbonate forms carbonic acid and sodium hydroxide. As a strong base, sodium hydroxide neutralizes gastric acid thereby acting as an antacid.
Rubidium Carbonate (Rb2CO3) is white in the form of powder. It is generally stable in the air and soluble in water.

questions.explanation: All except Lithium are soluble in water and stable to heat.

questions.text: Halogens are being used as fire extinguishers, mild antiseptic CFCs and many other organic chemicals, Which of the following halogen is used to kill the bacteria in drinking water.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Bromine is not typically used for disinfection of drinking water. It is more commonly employed in hot tubs and swimming pools as a disinfectant.
Fluorine gas is not used for disinfection of drinking water. Fluoride, an ion derived from fluorine, is sometimes added to drinking water, but for dental health rather than as a disinfectant.

One of the easiest and cost effective way to clean water is to add some amount of chlorine into it. But if chlorine is added in excess then the water becomes unfit for consumption. So, when chlorinated water from treatment plants is disposed into rivers etc then it becomes harmful for aquatic species as excess of chlorinated water can lead to death of living beings. Hence, chlorine must be removed from water before releasing the water from water treatment plants into rivers, lakes or oceans.

Iodine is used for disinfection, but it is less common in the treatment of drinking water. It is sometimes used in camping or emergency situations where other disinfection methods are not readily available.

questions.explanation: One of the easiest and cost effective way to clean water is to add some amount of chlorine into it. But if chlorine is added in excess then the water becomes unfit for consumption. So, when chlorinated water from treatment plants is disposed into rivers etc then it becomes harmful for aquatic species as excess of chlorinated water can lead to death of living beings. Hence, chlorine must be removed from water before releasing the water from water treatment plants into rivers, lakes or oceans.

questions.text: Which one of the following has the highest ionic character?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fluorine (F) is highly electronegative, and hydrogen (H) has a lower electronegativity. The electronegativity difference is relatively high, making HF a polar covalent molecule with some ionic character.
Sodium (Na) is a metal, and chlorine (Cl) is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference is relatively high, indicating a strong ionic character. NaCl is a classic example of an ionic compound.
Chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, but the electronegativity difference is not as high as in NaCl. HCl is a polar covalent molecule with some ionic character.
Cesium (Cs) is a metal, and fluorine (F) is a nonmetal. The electronegativity difference is relatively high, indicating a significant ionic character. CsF is an ionic compound.

questions.explanation: CsF has the highest ionic character because Cs is the most electropositive element and Fluorine is the most electronegative element. Hence the bond that would be formed between the most electropositive and the most electronegative elements would be very strong and will have the highest ionic character.

questions.text: Alkali metals cannot make carbides except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cesium (Cs) can indeed form carbides. Alkali metals, including cesium, react with carbon to produce cesium carbide (Cs₂C₂). The formation of carbides is more prominent for cesium compared to other alkali metals.
Sodium (Na) can also form carbides. The reaction of sodium with carbon, typically at elevated temperatures, leads to the formation of sodium carbide (Na₂C₂).
Lithium (Li) can form lithium carbide (Li₂C₂) under certain conditions. However, lithium carbides are less stable compared to those of other alkali metals.
Potassium (K) can form carbides as well. The reaction of potassium with carbon results in the formation of potassium carbide (K₄C₄).

questions.explanation: Li more closely resembles the metals of group 2 than the metals of its own group (1). It is more reactive with carbon, nitrogen and hydrogen and less reactive with water, oxygen and halogens. Li forms a carbide (Li2C2) similar to that of calcium.
